# Questions in Livestream 133

Live Stream #133: The ETS D&D Special Live Stream

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=4620" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:00</a> | What Greek textbook would he recommend? | He recommended William Mounce's introductory Greek textbook as a strong beginner and self-teaching option. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=4690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:10</a> | Did Leviticus 18:22 originally condone the act and later get redacted because of a cultural shift? | No. He argued the verse is framed by idolatry material and there is no evidence that it once condoned the act and was later revised. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=5048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:08</a> | Was Kiara Falk still doing producer work for the stream, or was he going solo? | She was still producing from the hotel setup, but device and Wi-Fi problems limited her normal online presence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=5190" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:30</a> | Did he attend the panel on Adam and Eve, and if so what were his thoughts? | He said he did not attend it because they arrived late the night before; he heard the time slot hurt attendance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=5267" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:47</a> | Will he do live D&D again? | Probably not soon; he described this as a special one-off treat, though another future event was possible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=5352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:12</a> | Why were the camera and connection worse than IP, David, and John's setup? | He was streaming from a hotel on an older laptop after the Chromebook did not work well with the hotel Wi-Fi. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=5447" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:47</a> | Were there any interesting ETS encounters, especially concerning members of AVR? | Yes. He described conversations with ETS attendees and scholars where the channel and prior debate clips had come up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=5796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:36</a> | How was ETS overall? | He said it was a good and enjoyable conference, especially for meeting people and having in-person conversations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=6165" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:45</a> | Did he get any new books besides the NET Bible? | Only a few. He mentioned buying a biblical archaeology book and seeing other unusual translations, but not buying many books. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=6343" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:43</a> | What was his favorite session or talk at ETS? | He singled out David Yoon's paper on semantic and exegetical errors in annihilationism, with another ritual-cups paper also standing out. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=6541" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:01</a> | In clan genealogy, are there ever disputes over who counts as the greatest member? | Yes. He said succession disputes and rival claims can happen, sometimes even splitting clans into new branches. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=6647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:47</a> | Were there any ETS talks he thought were just bad? | Yes. He singled out one paper on the Exodus as badly argued and said it mishandled both the text and Egyptological context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=7417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:37</a> | How did his meeting with David Wood go? | It was very brief; David Wood was leaving while he was entering, and IP still hoped to facilitate a fuller meeting later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=7647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:27</a> | Who is Pharaoh Sesostris? | He explained that Sesostris is a Greek form for the Senusret kings of Dynasty 12 in the Middle Kingdom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=8800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:40</a> | Did the earlier David Falk and Titus Kennedy debate come up at ETS? | Yes. He said students and scholars had been discussing clips from the debate, including the Amorites/Ammonites exchange. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/m0lfwar6BD0?t=9091" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:31</a> | Was there anything amusing or fun at ETS that he wanted to talk about? | He described enjoyable viewer meetings, Mr. EC photo moments, and a funny exchange where Steve Ortiz knew him before recognizing Michael Jones. |
